metadata.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Initial Management of Hypertension in Adults
metadata.scope: Adults (age 18+) with a new diagnosis of essential hypertension

recommendations.title: Patient Assessment
recommendations.content:
All patients presenting with elevated blood pressure should undergo the following assessment:
1. Confirm blood pressure reading with at least two measurements on separate occasions
2. Review current medication list, including over-the-counter medications
3. Assess for the presence of type 2 diabetes mellitus
4. Assess for the presence of chronic kidney disease
5. Obtain baseline laboratory studies including a basic metabolic panel

source_location.source_text: All patients presenting with elevated blood pressure should undergo the following assessment...

recommendations.title: Treatment Decision Principles — Stage 2 Hypertension
recommendations.content: Patients with Stage 2 hypertension (SBP >= 140) should begin pharmacological therapy regardless of comorbidity status.

source_location.source_text: Patients with Stage 2 hypertension (SBP >= 140) should begin pharmacological therapy regardless of comorbidity status.

recommendations.title: Treatment Decision Principles — Stage 1 with Comorbidities
recommendations.content: Patients with Stage 1 hypertension (SBP 130-139) AND diabetes or chronic kidney disease should begin pharmacological therapy due to elevated cardiovascular risk.

recommendations.title: Treatment Decision Principles — Stage 1 without Comorbidities
recommendations.content: Patients with Stage 1 hypertension without high-risk comorbidities should attempt lifestyle modification for 8 weeks before reassessment.

recommendations.title: First-Line Medication — Lisinopril
recommendations.content: Lisinopril (an ACE inhibitor) is recommended as the first-line agent due to its well-established efficacy, renal protective effects, and favorable side effect profile.
recommendations.rationale: Multiple meta-analyses confirm that ACE inhibitors reduce cardiovascular mortality and slow the progression of diabetic nephropathy and chronic kidney disease.

recommendations.title: Monitoring — ACE Inhibitor Therapy
recommendations.content: All patients starting ACE inhibitor therapy require a Basic Metabolic Panel (BMP) to monitor renal function and electrolytes.
remarks: Patients with chronic kidney disease require earlier laboratory follow-up (2 weeks) due to higher risk of ACE inhibitor-related changes in renal function.
Patients on lifestyle modification alone do not require routine laboratory monitoring beyond standard preventive care.

recommendations.title: Lifestyle Modification — Dietary Changes
recommendations.content: Adopt the DASH (Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension) diet, which emphasizes fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and low-fat dairy products while limiting saturated fat and sodium intake to less than 2,300 mg per day.

recommendations.title: Lifestyle Modification — Physical Activity
recommendations.content: Engage in at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity aerobic exercise, such as brisk walking, or 75 minutes per week of vigorous-intensity aerobic exercise.

recommendations.title: Lifestyle Modification — Weight Management
recommendations.content: Achieve and maintain a healthy body weight (BMI 18.5-24.9 kg/m2). Even modest weight loss of 5-10% can significantly reduce blood pressure.

recommendations.title: Lifestyle Modification — Smoking Cessation
recommendations.content: If applicable, provide smoking cessation counseling and resources.

recommendations.title: Clinical Workflow Integration
recommendations.content: This guideline is designed to be implemented through clinical decision support systems integrated into the electronic health record. Automated alerts should notify clinicians when a patient meets criteria for pharmacological intervention based on blood pressure readings and documented comorbidities.
recommendations.scope_notes: Implementation guidance — not a clinical recommendation. Describes how the guideline should be operationalized in health systems.

recommendations.title: Quality Measures
recommendations.content:
The following quality measures are recommended for monitoring guideline adherence:
- Percentage of patients with hypertension who have a blood pressure reading documented within the past 12 months
- Percentage of patients meeting treatment initiation criteria who received appropriate pharmacological therapy within 30 days
- Percentage of patients on ACE inhibitor therapy who received follow-up laboratory monitoring within the recommended timeframe
